mechanism_summary: ngram4 folds the last 4 edge IDs into the map index, rewarding novel path tuples through the conversion loop and retaining intermediate corpus states that build up surrogate-producing byte arrangements single-edge feedback discards.
one_line: ngram4 path-tuple retention builds surrogate-range code-unit inputs

mechanism_summary: ngram4's 4-edge path tuples through the multi-step encoding-detection if-cascade yield new-coverage signal at each step, retaining BOM-adjacent near-miss inputs that single-edge feedback collapses to already-seen buckets and discards.
one_line: ngram4 rewards encoding-cascade path tuples, retaining BOM near-miss inputs

mechanism_summary: ngram4 rewards the false-branch content-dispatch fan-out (END_TAG, PI, start-tag, char-data) as distinct 4-edge path tuples, retaining diverse non-CDATA seeds that single-edge feedback under-retains, keeping pressure off the CDATA true side.
one_line: ngram4 path-tuples retain non-CDATA content seeds via false-branch fan-out

mechanism_summary: ngram4's 4-edge path index makes the 3-byte UTF-8 decode block a distinct new bucket, retaining inputs carrying overlong-sequence byte patterns (0xe0 lead + two continuations) that single-edge feedback collapses and discards before val < 0x800 is reachable.
one_line: ngram4 bucket granularity retains overlong-3-byte UTF-8 sequence inputs
